docs.title: hole-orientation-comparison.jpg
docs.text: Source: Servo Magazine - "Maximizing Strength for Printing Combat Robots with a 3D Printer" URL: https://www.servomagazine.com/magazine/article/maximizing-strength-for-printing-combat-robots-with-a-3d-printer Direct Image URL: https://www.servomagazine.com/uploads/main/SV_0119_CZ_Clingman_Orientation-Comparison.jpg Description: Comparison diagram showing horizontal vs vertical hole orientation in 3D printed parts. Demonstrates why vertically-oriented holes are structurally superior to horizontally-oriented ones for stress-bearing features like weapon axles and motor mounts. License: Educational use - article content Usage: Module 6 (3D Printing) - demonstrating layer orientation principles

docs.title: Naked Singularity Beetleweight Robot
docs.text: Source: Instructables - "Naked Singularity Beetleweight Wedge Combat Robot" Author: User on Instructables URL: https://www.instructables.com/Naked-Singularity-Beetleweight-Wedge-Combat-Robot/ License: Instructables platform content (educational use) Usage: Module 8 (Assembly) - demonstrating mechanical assembly techniques

docs.title: frame-assembly.jpg
docs.text: Step: Internal Frame (Step 4) URL: https://content.instructables.com/F9P/T2D0/H21CGOK5/F9PT2D0H21CGOK5.jpg Description: Building the aluminum box structure with mandibles using cut aluminum bar pieces, drilling and tapping holes for fastening.

docs.title: armor-plates-cutting.jpg
docs.text: Step: Top and Bottom Armor Plates (Step 7) URL: https://content.instructables.com/FGY/C3CS/H21CGOK8/FGYC3CSH21CGOK8.jpg Description: Cutting protective armor from aluminum and UHMW plastic sheets using jigsaws and woodworking tools.

docs.title: side-bumpers-assembly.jpg
docs.text: Step: Side Bumpers (Step 8) URL: https://content.instructables.com/FQT/790R/H21CGOLD/FQT790RH21CGOLD.jpg Description: Manufacturing side bumpers from UHMW bar stock with homemade nutstrip fastening to armor plates.

docs.title: Robot Combat Wiki - 3D Printing
docs.text: URL: https://robotcombatwiki.com/wiki/3D_Printing Key Principle: "Orient parts so that the direction that needs to be the strongest lies horizontal during printing." Notes: Provides theoretical guidance on layer orientation but no visual diagrams.

docs.title: MatterHackers - Materials for Combat Robots
docs.text: URL: https://www.matterhackers.com/articles/5-materials-that-are-perfect-for-3d-printing-combat-robots Focus: Material selection (NylonX, Polycarbonate, PETG, ABS, TPU) Notes: No orientation diagrams, focused on filament properties.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• All images downloaded for educational use in CTRC BattleBots curriculum
  • Wiring and electronics photos were deliberately excluded per curriculum design
  • Focus maintained on mechanical assembly, 3D printing orientation, and weapon archetypes
  • Robot archetype images sourced from Creative Commons projects, educational resources, and product documentation
  • All motor reference materials are from manufacturer datasheets and open-source CAD models
  • Downloaded: March 12, 2026

docs.title: Resources
docs.text: A curated collection of external resources for VEX V5 robot design and Onshape CAD. These links supplement the learning course with additional tutorials, community knowledge, and vendor information.

docs.title: Official Onshape Resources
docs.text:
• Onshape Learning Center — Free structured courses directly from Onshape covering all skill levels
  • Onshape Help Documentation — Complete reference for every feature and tool in Onshape
  • Onshape YouTube Channel — Video tutorials and webinars
  • Onshape Forum — Community Q&A for Onshape-specific questions

docs.title: VEX-Specific Onshape Tutorials
docs.text:
• VEX V5 CAD Resources — Official VEX resources for robot design in Onshape
  • frcdesign.org — A well-known CAD learning resource that inspired this site (FRC-focused but many concepts transfer to VEX)

docs.title: Discussion Forums
docs.text:
• VEX Forum — The largest VEX community forum. Design discussion, robot reveals, strategy, and more. Essential reading for any serious VEX designer.
  • VEX Discord — Real-time chat with VEX community members. Has channels for CAD help, design discussion, and team collaboration.
  • r/vex on Reddit — VEX community on Reddit

docs.title: Technical Resources
docs.text:
• RobotEvents — Match data, event results, and match videos for every VEX event
  • VEX Via — Skills rankings and team statistics
  • VEX V5 Game Manual — Official rules (updated annually)

docs.title: Open-Source Robot Designs
docs.text:
Many top teams publish their robot CAD after the season. Study these to learn from the best:

  • Search VEX Forum for "robot reveal" or "CAD release" threads
  • Look for teams who share their entire design process publicly
  • Check team websites and GitHub pages for Onshape document links

Study Top Teams

Many top VEX teams are known for exceptional design and public knowledge sharing. Search for robot reveals and CAD releases on the VEX Forum to learn from the best.

docs.title: VEX-Specific Vendors
docs.text: Vendor Specialty Website VEX Robotics V5 motors, sensors, structure, electronics vexrobotics.com RoboSource VEX parts and accessories robosource.net McMaster-Carr Raw materials, fasteners, bearings, hardware mcmaster.com

docs.title: Raw Materials
docs.text: Supplier Products McMaster-Carr Aluminum tube/plate/sheet, fasteners, bearings, hardware OnlineMetals Aluminum stock, cut to size Metal Supermarkets Aluminum and steel stock, local pickup SendCutSend Laser/waterjet cutting services (upload DXF, receive parts)

docs.title: Design Calculators
docs.text:
• ReCalc — Robotics calculator for arms, elevators, drivetrains, flywheels, and pneumatics
  • JVN Design Calculator — Classic motor and mechanism calculator (useful for general robotics design)
  • Belt Calculator (WCP) — Calculate belt lengths for HTD belt systems

docs.title: CAD Utilities
docs.text:
• MKCAD — Robotics part library for Onshape (search in Onshape App Store)
  • FeatureScripts — Custom features in Onshape (Tube Converter, Shaft Generator, Belt & Chain, Gusset, Lighten)

docs.title: Video Resources
docs.text:
• VEX V5 CAD Tutorials — VEX CAD and design videos
  • VEX Robot Reveals — Annual robot reveal videos from VEX teams worldwide
  • VEXU and VRC Match Videos — Inside look at top VEX team robots

docs.title: Books and Guides
docs.text:
• "VEX V5 Mechanical Design Guide" — Community-maintained design reference (search VEX Forum)
  • "Shigley's Mechanical Engineering Design" — University-level reference for deeper mechanical engineering knowledge
  • "Engineering Design Process" — Understanding the formal design process

docs.title: Competitions and Challenges
docs.text:
• VEX Off-Season Events — Compete and test designs outside the main season
  • CAD Challenges — Community-run design challenges on VEX Forum and Discord
  • Early Season Builds — Teams that design and build a robot quickly after game reveal — study their rapid design process

docs.title: Software Tools
docs.text:
Tool Use Link Onshape Primary CAD tool onshape.com MeshLab STL viewing and repair meshlab.net Inkscape Vector graphics for laser cutting inkscape.org GitHub Version control for code and documentation github.com

This List is Not Exhaustive

The VEX community creates new resources constantly. Check VEX Forum and the VEX Discord for the latest tools, calculators, and tutorials. If you find a great resource not listed here, consider contributing to add it.
